title: Oil is re-accelerating even after the IEA’s record reserve release
summary: Reuters reported that oil rebounded as markets doubted whether the IEA’s planned 400 million-barrel strategic-reserve release could offset Iran-war supply risk, and separately that Iran’s military said the world should be prepared for $200 oil. The second point is rhetoric, not a price print, but together they mark a meaningful shift: the market is not treating policy action as enough to fully cap the shock.
